description:
You are helping a data-science team to pre-clean a large web-access log before it is loaded into a downstream analytics pipeline.  
    Everything will be done from the Linux terminal inside the running container.  
    The raw log for the day has already been placed at:

        /home/user/project/logs/raw/web_access_2023-11-05.log

    Goal
    ----
    1. Inspect the raw log and extract **only** the lines that simultaneously satisfy **all** of the following conditions  
       a. HTTP status code is 4xx or 5xx (≥ 400 and < 600).  
       b. Client IP **does not** start with “10.” (internal traffic must be excluded).  
       c. The request path contains the exact substring “/api/v1/order”.

    2. Store the kept lines in a *new* file named:

        /home/user/project/cleaned/critical_errors_2023-11-05.log

       • The directory `/home/user/project/cleaned/` does not yet exist; create it.  
       • Lines must be written **exactly as they appeared in the raw log** (no re-formatting),  
         but need to be:  
         – sorted in simple lexicographical order (`sort` default order);  
         – fully de-duplicated (no duplicate lines).

    3. Produce a one-line-per-code aggregated summary and save it as a CSV at:

        /home/user/project/cleaned/errors_summary_2023-11-05.csv

       Required CSV format (the automated test is strict):  
       • First line is the header, literally:  
         status_code,count  
       • Each following line contains one distinct status code and the number of
         occurrences in `critical_errors_2023-11-05.log`.  
       • Lines must be ordered in **ascending numeric** order of the status code.  
       • Fields are comma-separated, no spaces, no quotes, no extra columns.

    4. When you are done, print the following two things to STDOUT so the grader can
       quickly inspect your work:  
       • The full path of the cleaned log file.  
       • The first five lines of the CSV summary file (that will include the header
         plus up to the first four data rows).

    Hints (do NOT reveal commands directly)
    ---------------------------------------
    • You will very likely want to use a regular expression with a negative look-ahead
      (or grep ‑v) to exclude the internal “10.” addresses.  
    • Remember that the automated checker will read the two output files **and**
      verify that no extra lines slipped in or were dropped.  
    • File and directory names are case-sensitive.

    Verification checklist
    ----------------------
    The grader will confirm:  
    • The directory `/home/user/project/cleaned/` exists.  
    • File `/home/user/project/cleaned/critical_errors_2023-11-05.log` exists,
      is non-empty, and its contents are an **exact** subset of the raw log that
      matches the three selection rules, sorted and deduplicated.  
    • File `/home/user/project/cleaned/errors_summary_2023-11-05.csv` exists,
      obeys the precise CSV format, and the counts reconcile with the cleaned log.  
    • Exactly six unique data lines (plus header) are present in the CSV, matching
      the status codes found in the cleaned log.  
    • Your final console output shows the two requested snippets.

    Do not hard-code the expected answer: derive it from the log.  
    Begin whenever you are ready.  Good luck!
truth:
<!--
        Pre-populated raw log file and its precise content
        (never revealed to the agent, used only for automated grading)
    -->
    <initial_files>
        <file path="/home/user/project/logs/raw/web_access_2023-11-05.log"><![CDATA[
2023-11-05T08:12:01Z 10.0.0.5 GET /api/v1/order 404 NotFound
2023-11-05T08:15:34Z 203.0.113.45 GET /api/v1/order 200 OK
2023-11-05T09:02:17Z 198.51.100.23 POST /api/v1/order 500 InternalServerError
2023-11-05T09:15:42Z 10.10.10.10 GET /api/v1/order 500 InternalServerError
2023-11-05T09:20:03Z 203.0.113.45 GET /health 200 OK
2023-11-05T10:05:55Z 198.51.100.23 GET /api/v1/order 404 NotFound
2023-11-05T10:05:55Z 198.51.100.23 GET /api/v1/order 404 NotFound
2023-11-05T11:30:44Z 192.0.2.10 GET /api/v1/order 502 BadGateway
2023-11-05T11:45:00Z 172.16.0.8 GET /api/v1/order 403 Forbidden
2023-11-05T12:00:12Z 203.0.113.45 GET /reports 404 NotFound
2023-11-05T12:05:12Z 203.0.113.45 GET /api/v1/order 403 Forbidden
2023-11-05T12:30:00Z 203.0.113.46 GET /api/v1/order 400 BadRequest
2023-11-05T12:30:00Z 10.0.0.16 GET /api/v1/order 400 BadRequest
2023-11-05T12:45:00Z 198.18.0.1 GET /api/v1/order 200 OK
2023-11-05T13:00:00Z 203.0.113.47 POST /api/v1/order 201 Created
2023-11-05T13:30:00Z 203.0.113.45 GET /api/v1/order 503 ServiceUnavailable
]]></file>
    </initial_files>

    <!-- Expected end state after the agent completes the task -->
    <expected_files>
        <file path="/home/user/project/cleaned/critical_errors_2023-11-05.log"><![CDATA[
2023-11-05T09:02:17Z 198.51.100.23 POST /api/v1/order 500 InternalServerError
2023-11-05T10:05:55Z 198.51.100.23 GET /api/v1/order 404 NotFound
2023-11-05T11:30:44Z 192.0.2.10 GET /api/v1/order 502 BadGateway
2023-11-05T11:45:00Z 172.16.0.8 GET /api/v1/order 403 Forbidden
2023-11-05T12:05:12Z 203.0.113.45 GET /api/v1/order 403 Forbidden
2023-11-05T12:30:00Z 203.0.113.46 GET /api/v1/order 400 BadRequest
2023-11-05T13:30:00Z 203.0.113.45 GET /api/v1/order 503 ServiceUnavailable
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/project/cleaned/errors_summary_2023-11-05.csv"><![CDATA[
status_code,count
400,1
403,2
404,1
500,1
502,1
503,1
]]></file>
    </expected_files>

    <!-- STDOUT snippet the automated grader will look for at the very end -->
    <required_stdout>
        <line>/home/user/project/cleaned/critical_errors_2023-11-05.log</line>
        <line>status_code,count</line>
    </required_stdout>
